changes.value:
Build the frontend view system: provider, left nav, and command-based view switching.

## Key files
- `ui/src/types/kanban.ts` — add ViewDef TypeScript type
- `ui/src/lib/views-context.tsx` — ViewsProvider, useViews() hook
- `ui/src/components/left-nav.tsx` — icon nav generated from view registry
- `ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx` — add nav.view + per-view alias commands
- `ui/src/App.tsx` — wrap with ViewsProvider, add left nav, conditional view render

## ViewsProvider
- Fetches views via `invoke('list_views')` on mount
- Manages `activeViewId` state (defaults to first view)
- Exposes: `views`, `activeView`, `setActiveView`, `loading`

## Left nav
- Maps view registry to vertical icon strip (lucide icons by name)
- Click dispatches `nav.view` command with view name
- Active view highlighted
- Always visible, collapsed to icons

## Commands (added to AppShell globalCommands)
- `nav.view` — switch to named view
- `nav.board` — alias for `:view board`
- Per-view aliases generated from registry

## App.tsx changes
- Add ViewsProvider to provider stack (after SchemaProvider)
- Add LeftNav to layout (flex row: nav | content)
- Conditional render: activeView.kind === 'board' ? BoardView : placeholder

## Checklist
- [ ] Add ViewDef type to kanban.ts
- [ ] Create ViewsProvider with useViews() hook
- [ ] Create LeftNav component
- [ ] Add nav.view command to AppShell
- [ ] Integrate into App.tsx layout
- [ ] App launches with left nav showing board icon
- [ ] Clicking board icon (or :view board in palette) keeps board active
- [ ] Bottom bar / mode indicator shows active view name

#phase2-views
Frontend ViewsProvider, left nav, and view switching

#phase2-views
\ No newline at end of file
+Build the frontend view system: provider, left nav, command-based view switching, and data-driven command attachment.

## Key files
- `ui/src/types/kanban.ts` — add ViewDef, ViewCommandDef TypeScript types
- `ui/src/lib/views-context.tsx` — ViewsProvider, useViews() hook
- `ui/src/components/left-nav.tsx` — icon nav generated from view registry
- `ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx` — add nav.view + per-view alias commands
- `ui/src/App.tsx` — wrap with ViewsProvider, add left nav, conditional view render
- `ui/src/components/board-view.tsx` — refactor to read commands from ViewDef

## Command Attachment Pattern
This is the key design: ViewDef declares commands as data (id, name, keys), renderers attach execute functions.

```typescript
// In BoardView (or any view renderer):
const executeMap: Record<string, () => void> = {
  "board.newCard": () => { /* add card logic */ },
  "board.collapseAll": () => { /* collapse logic */ },
  "board.expandAll": () => { /* expand logic */ },
};

// Merge ViewDef command metadata with local execute fns
const commands: CommandDef[] = viewDef.commands.map(cmd => ({
  id: cmd.id,
  name: cmd.name,
  keys: cmd.keys,
  execute: executeMap[cmd.id],
  available: cmd.id in executeMap, // unavailable if no implementation
}));

// Register via CommandScopeProvider
<CommandScopeProvider commands={commands}>
  {/* view content */}
</CommandScopeProvider>
```

Flow:
1. ViewsProvider loads ViewDefs from backend (includes command metadata)
2. Active view renders (e.g. BoardView)
3. BoardView reads commands from its ViewDef
4. Creates CommandDef[] by merging YAML metadata + local execute fns
5. Wraps children in CommandScopeProvider
6. Palette sees view commands via scope chain
7. On view switch: old scope exits, new scope enters

## ViewsProvider
- Fetches views via `invoke('list_views')` on mount
- Manages `activeViewId` state (defaults to first view)
- Exposes: `views`, `activeView`, `setActiveView`, `loading`

## Left nav
- Maps view registry to vertical icon strip (lucide icons by name)
- Click dispatches `nav.view` command with view name
- Active view highlighted
- Always visible, collapsed to icons

## Checklist
- [ ] Add ViewDef, ViewCommandDef types to kanban.ts
- [ ] Create ViewsProvider with useViews() hook
- [ ] Create LeftNav component
- [ ] Add nav.view command + per-view aliases to AppShell
- [ ] Refactor BoardView to read commands from ViewDef and attach implementations
- [ ] Integrate into App.tsx layout (ViewsProvider + LeftNav + conditional render)
- [ ] App launches with left nav showing board icon
- [ ] :view board in palette works
- [ ] View commands appear in palette with correct names/keys from YAML

#phase2-views
